title: Decision
description: A lightweight decision in the open-decision register. Promote to an ADR when architecturally significant (see governance.md). IDs follow the DEC- scheme. Decision statuses are exactly Proposed/Approved/Rejected/Superseded/Deferred.

title.description: Short imperative title of the decision.

context.description: The situation/problem that forces a decision.

recommendation.description: The recommended option and the gist of why.

rationale.description: Reasoning behind the recommendation; the evidence and trade-offs.

status.description: Decision lifecycle status. EXACTLY these five values (governance.md core safeguard: never render Proposed as Approved).

decided_by.description: Who approved/rejected (human or authorized gate). Set when status leaves Proposed.

decided_on.description: ISO date the decision reached its current terminal status.

superseded_by.description: DEC- that replaces this one. Set on the old item when superseded.

promoted_to_adr.description: If promoted, the ADR id this decision became (record the promotion so the link is never lost).

source.description: Provenance: input section (a known decision) or the derived stage that raised it.
